title: Mastery Exam: Accessible CSS under Real Preferences
summary: Mastery Exam: Accessible CSS under Real Preferences retrieves prior skills in an original independent CSS accessibility regression decision scenario and requires implementation, behavior, failure-state, and transfer evidence.
objectives: Apply css zoom reflow, css visible hidden content, css focus visibility, css user preferences, css touch targets, css accessibility regression in unfamiliar constraints.
Produce or audit a real independent CSS accessibility regression decision artifact without happy-path-only reasoning.
Preserve earlier requirements while testing keyboard, narrow-width, invalid, and assistive-semantic states.

starterFiles.html: <!-- Continue the Volunteer Shift Deck accessibility hardening here. -->
starterFiles.css: /* Continue the accessible CSS regression system here. */

steps.title: 01 · predict 1: shift selection at four-hundred-percent zoom · quiz
steps.instruction: Checkpoint 1: Which claim survives the changed case? Scenario: independent CSS accessibility regression decision.
steps.why: Mastery Exam: Accessible CSS under Real Preferences makes the decision observable and testable. This is checkpoint 1 in independent CSS accessibility regression decision.

content.title: independent CSS accessibility regression decision evidence
content.text: Choose the claim that preserves purpose, native behavior, and understandable relationships when presentation changes.

hints: Mastery Exam: Accessible CSS under Real Preferences checkpoint 1: identify the user task and data relationship first.
Mastery Exam: Accessible CSS under Real Preferences checkpoint 1: compare source, native behavior, and announced meaning.
Mastery Exam: Accessible CSS under Real Preferences checkpoint 1: make the smallest repair that preserves earlier passing work.

options.text: Independent CSS accessibility regression decision case 1: a desktop layout can remain fixed because browser zoom simply scales the screenshot.

Independent CSS accessibility regression decision case 1: test complete workflows at high zoom and narrow effective widths, then remove fixed dimensions, clipping, and nonwrapping assumptions that lose content.
Independent CSS accessibility regression decision case 1: disable browser zoom with viewport metadata so the desktop layout remains stable.

hints: Mastery Exam: Accessible CSS under Real Preferences checkpoint 2: identify the user task and data relationship first.
Mastery Exam: Accessible CSS under Real Preferences checkpoint 2: compare source, native behavior, and announced meaning.
Mastery Exam: Accessible CSS under Real Preferences checkpoint 2: make the smallest repair that preserves earlier passing work.

options.text: independent CSS accessibility regression decision phase 2.1: define whether content should render occupy space receive focus accept hits and remain exposed
independent CSS accessibility regression decision phase 2.2: choose the technique matching all five states
independent CSS accessibility regression decision phase 2.3: test keyboard accessibility tree pointer and layout behavior
independent CSS accessibility regression decision phase 2.4: exercise reveal removal animation and stale-focus transitions

steps.title: 03 · inspect 3: shift action focus disappearance incident · quiz
steps.instruction: Checkpoint 3: Which claim survives the changed case? Scenario: independent CSS accessibility regression decision.
steps.why: Mastery Exam: Accessible CSS under Real Preferences makes the decision observable and testable. This is checkpoint 3 in independent CSS accessibility regression decision.

content.title: independent CSS accessibility regression decision evidence
content.text: Choose the claim that preserves purpose, native behavior, and understandable relationships when presentation changes.

hints: Mastery Exam: Accessible CSS under Real Preferences checkpoint 3: identify the user task and data relationship first.
Mastery Exam: Accessible CSS under Real Preferences checkpoint 3: compare source, native behavior, and announced meaning.
Mastery Exam: Accessible CSS under Real Preferences checkpoint 3: make the smallest repair that preserves earlier passing work.

options.text: Independent CSS accessibility regression decision case 3: a subtle color shift is enough focus evidence for every control.
Independent CSS accessibility regression decision case 3: use a distinct boundary with thickness and offset, prevent clipping and overlay obstruction, and test every target across surfaces and modes.
Independent CSS accessibility regression decision case 3: remove all outlines and rely on the browser status bar for links.

steps.title: 04 · answer 4: shift deck motion contrast and color-scheme preference review · quiz
steps.instruction: Checkpoint 4: Which claim survives the changed case? Scenario: independent CSS accessibility regression decision.

content.title: independent CSS accessibility regression decision evidence
content.text: Choose the claim that preserves purpose, native behavior, and understandable relationships when presentation changes.

hints: Mastery Exam: Accessible CSS under Real Preferences checkpoint 4: identify the user task and data relationship first.
Mastery Exam: Accessible CSS under Real Preferences checkpoint 4: compare source, native behavior, and announced meaning.
Mastery Exam: Accessible CSS under Real Preferences checkpoint 4: make the smallest repair that preserves earlier passing work.

options.text: Independent CSS accessibility regression decision case 4: preference media queries should disable all useful feedback and state changes.

Independent CSS accessibility regression decision case 4: identify the effect causing discomfort or reduced access, replace it with a stable equivalent, and verify meaning across each supported preference.
Independent CSS accessibility regression decision case 4: invert every color for dark mode without rechecking contrast or imagery.

Mastery Exam: Accessible CSS under Real Preferences checkpoint 5: make the smallest repair that preserves earlier passing work.

options.text: Independent CSS accessibility regression decision case 5: small icons are usable when their SVG graphic is visually sharp.

Independent CSS accessibility regression decision case 5: size the interactive area rather than only the glyph, separate adjacent targets, and provide simple pointer and keyboard alternatives to complex gestures.
Independent CSS accessibility regression decision case 5: make icons eight pixels wide and add a tooltip for users who miss them.

options.text: Independent CSS accessibility regression decision case 6: accessibility needs to be checked only once after all visual work is finished.

Independent CSS accessibility regression decision case 6: attach a risk-based automated and manual matrix to each cumulative build, run it after changes, and record evidence plus affected-path retests.
Independent CSS accessibility regression decision case 6: wait until certification day so accessibility checks cover the final code only once.

independent CSS accessibility regression decision phase 7.2: increase zoom and narrow effective width without changing content
independent CSS accessibility regression decision phase 7.3: trace clipping overlap hidden controls and two-dimensional scrolling
independent CSS accessibility regression decision phase 7.4: repair intrinsic constraints and repeat keyboard plus pointer workflows

options.text: Independent CSS accessibility regression decision case 8: opacity zero removes an element from keyboard and accessibility navigation.

Independent CSS accessibility regression decision case 8: state visual, layout, focus, hit-test, and accessibility intent first, then choose hidden, display, visibility, opacity, or a tested visually hidden pattern.
Independent CSS accessibility regression decision case 8: use visibility hidden for skip links and reveal them only after activation.

Mastery Exam: Accessible CSS under Real Preferences checkpoint 28: make the smallest repair that preserves earlier passing work.

options.text: Independent CSS accessibility regression decision case 28: preference media queries should disable all useful feedback and state changes.

Independent CSS accessibility regression decision case 28: identify the effect causing discomfort or reduced access, replace it with a stable equivalent, and verify meaning across each supported preference.
Independent CSS accessibility regression decision case 28: invert every color for dark mode without rechecking contrast or imagery.

steps.title: 29 · answer 29: shift action accidental-activation review · quiz
steps.instruction: Checkpoint 29: Which claim survives the changed case? Scenario: independent CSS accessibility regression decision.
steps.why: Mastery Exam: Accessible CSS under Real Preferences makes the decision observable and testable. This is checkpoint 29 in independent CSS accessibility regression decision.

options.text: Independent CSS accessibility regression decision case 29: small icons are usable when their SVG graphic is visually sharp.

Independent CSS accessibility regression decision case 29: size the interactive area rather than only the glyph, separate adjacent targets, and provide simple pointer and keyboard alternatives to complex gestures.
Independent CSS accessibility regression decision case 29: make icons eight pixels wide and add a tooltip for users who miss them.

options.text: Independent CSS accessibility regression decision case 30: accessibility needs to be checked only once after all visual work is finished.

Independent CSS accessibility regression decision case 30: attach a risk-based automated and manual matrix to each cumulative build, run it after changes, and record evidence plus affected-path retests.
Independent CSS accessibility regression decision case 30: wait until certification day so accessibility checks cover the final code only once.
